Vue vs React: What's Actually Different, and How to Pick One (Plus Common Interview Questions)

What's actually different between Vue and React? This post starts from where each framework came from and what it was designed for, covers the framework-vs-library distinction, then compares ecosystem, learning curve and debugging to explain why React ended up more popular — handy for interviews too.
Some Sleep Myths and the Importance of Sleep Quality

Many of my students and coworkers have had sleep issues, including people close to me. So I've done a fair amount of reading and trial. Since a lot of people keep asking lately, here's a quick share on what I learned.
Why Am I a Coach Again for the 2024 Node.js Enterprise Capstone Program?!

I was already a capstone coach last year, but I'm honestly a bit surprised to have the "honor" of being part of the whole program again this year. So to debrief myself, I'm writing this post to record the process and my takeaways. Hopefully it helps future students understand what this program is about. Also, this is for my students.
Zero-Width Space (ZWSP): The Invisible Character That Breaks Your Code

Ever had a string that looks like plain "ABC" or "123" on screen but won't run as code or won't post as a URL? That's often a "ghost character" — specifically the zero-width space. This post covers what zero-width space (ZWSP) is, why it sneaks into your code, and how to find and remove it before it eats your afternoon.
A Few Filter Tricks: Drop All the Falsy Values in One Line

We reach for JavaScript's array `filter` method all the time to pull out the elements we care about — say, every number bigger than 10. It's one of the most-used array methods out there. In this post I'll show a few small tricks for filtering out falsy values, plus how to do the same thing when you're working with arrays of objects.
How to Stop Users From Selecting Specific Elements
Sometimes you want a specific section on your site to be unselectable by the user. Here are a few ways to do that.
Team Communication: Resolving Conflict, Building Consensus and Trust

Anywhere people work together, communication friction is inevitable. This post is my experience on how to defuse it — understanding the other side's perspective, when to bring in a neutral mediator, building consensus and trust through 1:1 conversation, picking the right way to deliver solutions (group announcement vs. DM vs. meeting), and giving people time to adjust afterwards.
Generate a UUID Right in the Browser — No npm Package Needed

When you're writing a blog post or a quick demo, you almost always end up faking some data — and that fake data usually needs a unique value, the classic key/id. This post walks through how to generate a unique key/id without pulling in any package at all, using the browser's built-in UUID support.
Discord .well-known Domain Verification Doesn't Work on GitHub Pages — Here's the Fix

I tried to set up Discord's HTTPS domain verification on my GitHub Pages-hosted Hexo blog and hit a wall — GitHub Pages flat-out ignores .well-known directories. This post walks through what went wrong, why Hexo also needed a tweak to even generate the .well-known folder, and the workaround that actually got Discord to verify: spinning up a dedicated .well-known repo as a separate GitHub Pages site.
Monorepo? Another Way to Manage Projects — Building a Vue3 Monorepo With Vite + pnpm

What is a Monorepo? How is it different from a regular project setup? I had a need for one recently, so I wrote this up. The post walks through Monolith vs Multi vs Monorepo, plus a hands-on Vue3 Monorepo build using Vite + pnpm.
